work_unit: frontend/aiStore.ts — FE-H-002 prompt injection bypass fix (immutable security directive in DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT)
situation: audit-2026-07-07-030 derived_fixes pending: FE-H-002 (high) prompt injection can bypass medium-risk tool confirm because DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS.allowMediumWithoutConfirm=true and no immutable system prompt segment to resist jailbreak. allowMediumWithoutConfirm:false already set on opt-frontend-1783366395 by prior round; missing second layer (system prompt hardening).
action: Appended [IMMUTABLE SECURITY DIRECTIVE] block to DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT in aiStore.ts. Block explicitly forbids skipping medium/high-risk confirmation regardless of user instruction, names common injection phrases ('skip confirmation', 'ignore prior rules', 'override permissions', 'execute without asking'), declares itself non-overridable. Complements allowMediumWithoutConfirm:false default.
outcome_evidence: commit a0332f4 on auto/opt-frontend-1783366395 pushed to origin (db2a4bd..a0332f4). git diff shows +5/-1 lines in aiStore.ts adding IMMUTABLE SECURITY DIRECTIVE block after existing rules section.
next_suggestion: 派生下一个 pending derived_fix: FE-M-001 (Vite dev server binds 0.0.0.0 without host check) — vite.config.ts 单文件 route-fix
FE-M-002 (no CSP header/meta) — index.html 单文件 route-fix
FE-M-003 (localStorage stores API key plaintext) — aiStore.ts persist partialize 改动

audit_findings.finding: DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS.allowMediumWithoutConfirm:true allows LLM-driven medium-risk tool execution without user confirmation, enabling prompt injection attacks to trigger state-changing operations (order placement, position deletion) via crafted user messages that instruct the model to skip confirmation.
audit_findings.remediation: Set allowMediumWithoutConfirm:false as default + append immutable system prompt segment that explicitly forbids skipping confirmation regardless of user instruction. Defense-in-depth: even if user toggles setting back to true via AiSettings, system prompt provides second layer against injection.
